headline: Lighter protocol discovered; governance token and contract scope identified; deployed address discovery incomplete
short_headline: Lighter ZK-rollup protocol scope documented; addresses pending

findings.text: No addresses found via block explorer name-tag search for 'Lighter' or 'LIT' on Ethereum Etherscan or Arbitrum Arbiscan.
No deployed contracts documentation page located at docs.lighter.xyz; docs cover technical architecture but not address listings.

Audit scope identified: ZkLighter.sol, AdditionalZkLighter.sol, Governance.sol, UpgradeGatekeeper.sol, and proxy contracts (1655 LoC total). Audits by Nethermind (Sept 2025), Block (April, Aug, Oct 2025). No audit-era addresses extracted yet.
No GitHub repository linked in protocol metadata; cannot walk Foundry/Hardhat deployment artifacts.
Protocol chains are Ethereum and Arbitrum; Ethereum smart contracts hold deposits; Arbitrum mentioned as secondary deployment but no addresses found on either chain.
No factory contracts identified to enumerate child pools/markets.
Identified privileged roles from audit: Network Governor, Validators, Sequencer, Security Council, Upgrade Gatekeeper, Treasury/Insurance Fund Operators. Roles documented but not mapped to addresses.
LIT governance token found on Ethereum at 0x232ce3bd40fcd6f80f3d55a522d03f25df784ee2 (ERC-20, verified).

rationale.verdict: Lighter's protocol structure and audit scope are documented, but deployed contract addresses on Ethereum and Arbitrum could not be discovered from public sources (block explorers, docs, audits, or GitHub). The LIT token on Ethereum is confirmed. Further investigation requires either direct GitHub access, protocol dashboard, or block-explorer name-tag registration for the protocol team.

evidence.shows: Links to 6 audit reports (Nethermind LighterCore Sept 2025, Nethermind EvmDepositBridge Sept 2025, Block April 2025, Block+Delta Aug 2025, Wrapper Oct 2025, Desert Exit Nov 2025, Spot Nov 2025) documenting contract scope.

Nethermind NM-0560 audit of ZkLighter core contracts (1655 LoC); lists 20 in-scope files including ZkLighter.sol, AdditionalZkLighter.sol, Governance.sol, UpgradeGatekeeper.sol, proxy contracts, verifier interfaces. Identifies privileged roles: Network Governor, Validators, Sequencer, Security Council, Upgrade Gatekeeper, Treasury/Insurance Fund Operators. Mentions USDC collateral and desert mode emergency mechanism.
Lighter homepage describes protocol as zero-knowledge rollup on Ethereum with L2 optimization; users deposit USDC; footnote mentions whitepaper, audits, docs, testnet. No deployed addresses listed.

Technical documentation for Lighter; covers architecture (Smart contracts on Ethereum hold deposits, Sequencer off-chain, periodic state commits, Desert Mode emergency mechanism). No addresses or deployment table found.
LIT token ERC-20 on Ethereum mainnet; name 'Lighter', symbol not visible in snippet but contract verified; 1,000,000,000 token supply mentioned.

unknowns: D1: Block explorer search for 'Lighter' name tag on Etherscan and Arbiscan returned no results; protocol team may not have registered tags.
D2: Deployed contracts documentation page not found at docs.lighter.xyz or derivative paths; may require GitHub repository or direct dashboard access.
D4: GitHub repository URL not provided in protocol.github field; cannot walk Foundry broadcast or Hardhat deployment artifacts.
D5-Ethereum: No ZkLighter or AdditionalZkLighter proxy/implementation addresses found on Ethereum mainnet.
D5-Arbitrum: Protocol mentions Arbitrum as secondary chain but no contract addresses discoverable.
D6: Factory pattern not yet identified; all pool/market enumeration methods unknown.
D7: Privileged role addresses (Network Governor, Validators, Treasury, Insurance Fund Operators) not extracted; require on-chain read or GitHub config.

protocol_metadata.about: Lighter is a zero-knowledge rollup on Ethereum optimized for perpetual futures and spot trading. It uses custom ZK circuits to verify order matching and liquidations, enabling scalability while maintaining Ethereum security guarantees. Users deposit USDC collateral and trade leveraged perpetual contracts with verifiable matching and automatic liquidation mechanisms, backed by an insurance fund. A Desert Mode emergency exit ensures users can withdraw even if the sequencer becomes unresponsive.
